author: TNN
title: Tension Eases as Encroachment Near Mauganj Temple is Cleared | Bhopal News - Times of India
text: Bhopal: After nearly six days of tense standoff, the administration on Saturday removed alleged encroachment from a portion of land near a temple in Devra village, Mauganj district. Local BJP MLA Pradeep Patel remains under house arrest at the Nai Garhi resthouse in Mauganj, which has been converted into a makeshift jail. According to locals, around 8:30 AM, administration teams arrived with JCB machines and demolished a boundary wall built on the disputed land, reclaiming about one acre of the nine-acre encroached area. \"Police are stationed in the village, and while the situation is tense, but it is under control,\" a villager said. An official, speaking on the condition of anonymity, confirmed that around 200 police personnel have been deployed in the village since Wednesday. The administration teams dismantled the boundary wall erected on the encroached land. Notices for eviction have been served to those living on the land, and due legal process will be followed, the official added. The conflict began when MLA Patel attempted to remove encroachment near the temple, allegedly by members of another community, sparking tensions. In response, the administration issued prohibitory orders. Patel was initially placed under house arrest in Rewa on Wednesday. After his release, he attempted to revisit the disputed site, but was intercepted by police and taken to the Nai Garhi resthouse, where he has been detained since. On Friday, when officials attempted to enter his room, Patel initially refused to open the door. However, after intervention from the Mangawan MLA, he eventually opened the door. The dispute revolves around alleged encroachment on land near the Devra Mahadevan temple.
